meta.title: Festivales Náuticos MX
meta.description: Festivales Náuticos MX, an organization based in Los Cabos, Mexico, that hosts nautical events such as the Nautical Day of the Dead Parade and the Christmas nautical festival "Mar y Christmas".

banner.headline: Experience the magic of the sea and Mexican tradition at Festivales Náuticos MX.

about.paragraph: Our festivals blend the majesty of the sea with Mexico's cultural richness to create unforgettable experiences.

altares.title: Altares en el Mar 2025
altares.info: “Altares en el Mar” is an immersive festival that honors our loved ones with floating altars decorated with flowers, candles and traditional offerings.
altares.cta: Relive the most spectacular moments from last year's edition.

marxmas.title: Mar & Xmas 2025
marxmas.info: “Mar & Xmas” celebrates the holiday season with illuminated parades, music and joy by the sea.
marxmas.cta: Discover how we celebrate Christmas at sea.

tourism.text: Los Cabos is an incomparable destination where the desert meets the sea, offering adventure, gastronomy, and culture.

gastronomia.title: Gastronomic Experiences
gastronomia.0: San José del Cabo Organic Market: open seasonally (November–May), with fresh produce, cheeses, breads, and local cuisine.
gastronomia.1: Seafood and street taco routes in Cabo San Lucas and San José: from food carts to family-run eateries.
gastronomia.2: Beach dinners: restaurants set up tables by the sea for romantic experiences.
gastronomia.3: Signature restaurants: renowned chefs such as Enrique Olvera (Manta, at The Cape) and Javier Plascencia with contemporary Mexican cuisine proposals.
gastronomia.4: Todos Santos: a foodie destination with cafés, artisan bakeries, and organic cuisine.
gastronomia.5: Sabores de Baja Festival (in Miraflores, near Los Cabos): celebrates traditional Baja California Sur cuisine with local products.

cultura.title: Culture, History & Towns
cultura.0: San José del Cabo: historic center with art galleries, colonial architecture, and the Art District.
cultura.1: Todos Santos (Pueblo Mágico): bohemian, with galleries, surfing at Cerritos Beach, and the famous “Hotel California.”
cultura.2: La Paz (capital of BCS): boardwalk, calm beaches, and a relaxed atmosphere.
cultura.3: Mission of San José del Cabo Añuití and Mission of Santiago: colonial landmarks.

naturaleza.title: Natural Attractions
naturaleza.0: El Arco of Cabo San Lucas: iconic rock formation where the Sea of Cortez meets the Pacific Ocean.
naturaleza.1: Lover’s Beach and Divorce Beach: contrasting the calm of the inner sea and the strength of the Pacific.
naturaleza.2: Chileno Bay and Santa Maria Bay: ideal for snorkeling and diving thanks to their crystal-clear waters.
naturaleza.3: Cabo Pulmo: National Park and World Heritage Site with the only living coral reef in the Gulf of California.
naturaleza.4: Sierra de la Laguna National Park: mountains, waterfalls, and hiking trails with great biodiversity.
naturaleza.5: Desert and dunes of Baja California Sur: contrasting landscapes with cacti and sea views.
naturaleza.6: El Médano Beach: the most popular in Cabo San Lucas, with a tourist atmosphere and water sports.
naturaleza.7: Palmilla Beach: calm and family-friendly in San José del Cabo.
naturaleza.8: Balandra Beach (La Paz, 2 hours away): considered one of the most beautiful in Mexico.
naturaleza.9: Costa Azul Beach: famous for surfing.

sponsorship.heading: Join as a sponsor
sponsorship.info: Be part of a festival that connects brands with audiences passionate about the sea and tradition.

gallery.heading: Past events gallery
gallery.text: See how we've transformed the sea into a stage of light and color.
